summary: PoC package executes arbitrary shell commands on install and hijacks the 'node' binary
details: Package self-identifies as a 'BunnyHijack PoC' demonstrating install-time arbitrary code execution. On `npm install`, `scripts.postinstall` runs `bun shim.js || node shim.js`, which executes platform-specific shell commands via `execSync` (launches the OS calculator via `gnome-calculator` / `calc` / `open -a Calculator`, opens a browser to a GitHub URL), and writes a marker file to `/tmp/.bun-npm-pwned`. Additionally, `package.json` declares `bin: { node: "./shim.js" }`, registering a `node` executable in `node_modules/.bin` that shadows the real Node.js runtime — subsequent invocations of `node` resolved through the local `.bin` directory will execute the package's shim instead. Although the demonstrated payload is benign (a calculator and marker file), the installer's machine executes attacker-authored code automatically at install time and any tool resolving `node` through the project's `.bin` is hijacked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
